title: phlox lutescens
intro: Phlox lutescens, also known as Wood's Phlox or Phlox divaricata, is a perennial flowering plant native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlox lutescens include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and calming anxiety, making it a popular herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Phlox lutescens can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat skin irritations, colds, and fever.
